title: AuthProvider pre-computes header values at construction time: fail-fast is correct and must be preserved
description: BearerAuth and BasicAuth pre-compute HeaderValue at construction time, so auth_header() is infallible and cannot fail at the first request. BearerAuth rejects empty and whitespace-only tokens; BasicAuth rejects usernames containing ':' (RFC 7617 section 2). This is correct: auth misconfiguration should fail loudly at startup, not silently on the first network call; auth_header() returns Option not Result, so making validation lazy would require every call site to handle an error that should never occur at runtime. A future contributor might suggest moving validation to auth_header() to simplify new() or allow lazy construction. That turns a startup-time configuration error into a silent per-request failure. The pre-computation also avoids a per-request allocation. Tested in bearer_auth_empty_token_rejected, bearer_auth_whitespace_only_token_rejected, basic_auth_colon_in_username_rejected.
